news.title: Treasury Market Liquidity Since April 2025
news.desc: New York Fed Liberty Street Economics reviews how U.S. Treasury market liquidity has evolved over the past year amid economic and political developments, including the impact of QT and TGA fluctuations on system reserve levels.

news.title: Fed's Logan Proposes Reducing Bank Reserve Demand to Shrink Balance Sheet Without Tightening Liquidity
news.desc: Dallas Fed President Lorie Logan proposed adjusting regulatory rules to lower banks' reserve demand as an alternative path for balance sheet reduction, avoiding a return to reserve scarcity — a key signal for USD liquidity outlook in Q2 2026.

news.title: Reflections on the Early Days of Reserve Management Purchases — New York Fed
news.desc: NY Fed speech highlighting that TGA rebuild and cumulative QT runoff brought system liquidity to the lowest levels since balance sheet reduction began, with reserves + ON RRP approaching critical scarcity thresholds near $2.3 trillion.
